competitive_position: Zycus is a top-two finalist competing head-to-head against one unnamed vendor. Coupa is the incumbent being replaced; GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba are the broader competitive set. NetSuite integration capability (prebuilt iSaaS middleware with 1,100+ APIs) is a confirmed wedge that advanced Zycus to the top two. Competitor identity and Coupa exit reasons are unknown, limiting tailored positioning. Recommended move: surface competitor identity and Coupa pain points explicitly in next buyer conversation to unlock finalist-specific differentiation.

champion.source: Avoma demo call 2026-04-20; Contact Roles; Next_Step__c 2026-06-29
champion.summary: Regina Oliver (Head of Procurement, Operations) is the primary champion candidate. She led the 2026-04-20 demo call, articulated the CRO spend pain ($500k), and is advancing Zycus through the RFP evaluation (Contact Role: Evaluator). However, her champion strength is developing, not strong: no evidence of active internal advocacy (EB introduction, competitive coaching, internal scorecards shared). Michelle (Director of Procurement) communicated the top-two finalist outcome but has not appeared on any call, so her engagement level and advocacy are unknown. Recommended move: deepen Regina's advocacy by co-building the quantified ROI case with her (making her the co-author and internal sponsor) and request an introduction to Michelle for a direct conversation on competitive positioning and EB access.

items.quote: The delay is because they had to make a 'netsuite decision' which we offered to help with
No CFO, VP Finance, or C-level budget authority identified or engaged across Contact Roles, Avoma calls, or Tasks
Zycus has been selected as a top two finalist

items.area: CIO engagement and IT approval process
items.quote: Ian Rosenblum (CIO) in Contact Roles but no evidence of participation on any call or task; new IT leadership and knowledge transfer in progress

recommended_moves: Map and engage the Economic Buyer (CFO or VP Finance/Operations) immediately once buyer completes internal NetSuite decision and IT knowledge transfer. Request executive connect to validate the business case, secure budget approval, and advance to commercial discussions. Without EB identification and engagement, pricing approval and contracting cannot proceed.

Co-build a quantified ROI and business case with Regina Oliver, anchored to the $500k CRO spend baseline she articulated on the 2026-04-20 demo. Model savings, cycle-time reduction, and procurement efficiency gains specific to the relevant stakeholder category. Make Regina the co-author and internal sponsor of the business case so she can advocate for Zycus to the EB.

Deliver detailed pricing breakdowns for the full S2P scope (iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) within 3 to 5 days of buyer completing their internal NetSuite decision and IT knowledge transfer. Include term-length options, services vs. SaaS breakdown, AI/Merlin pricing, and implementation timeline. Pricing delivery is the unlock to EB approval and contracting.
next_step: JRA 6/29: Email from Michelle, Director of Procurement. Zycus has been selected as a top two finalist for their S2P transformation project. Next steps could include an additional demo, pricing breakdowns, and implementation discussions. The delay is because they had to make a "netsuite decision" which we offered to help with. Their timeline remains tight because of the Coupa replace.Additionally, there is new IT leadership. Jason, project lead is currently facilitating a knowledge transfer of the project before we move forward with next steps.

sources: Contact Roles: Michelle (Director of Procurement, last name unknown), Regina Oliver (Head of Procurement, Operations), Ian Rosenblum (CIO), Jess Marenco (Associate Director, Accounting Operations), Anthony Morano (Head of Solution Design and Delivery) — none with explicit 'Economic Buyer' or 'Executive Sponsor' role designation
No Avoma call with CFO, VP Finance, or C-level budget authority participation
No Task or Event with CFO, VP Finance, or executive sponsor in subject or description within 90-day window
Next_Step__c 2026-06-29: finalist email from Michelle (Director of Procurement), no EB named
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged across all sources. Michelle (Director of Procurement) sent the finalist email (2026-06-29, source: Next_Step__c), but Director-level procurement typically does not hold final budget authority for a $225k SaaS purchase at a clinical-stage biopharma. The EB is likely the CFO or VP Finance/Operations. Ian Rosenblum (CIO) is in the Contact Roles but has not appeared on any call or task, and CIO authority over a procurement-led S2P buy is uncertain without direct evidence. Regina Oliver (Head of Procurement, Operations) is engaged but is the evaluator, not the budget holder. The EB gap is the single highest forecast risk: commercial sign-off, pricing approval, and contracting cannot proceed without EB identification and engagement. The recommended move is for the deal owner's manager to request an executive connect with the CFO or VP Finance/Operations within 5 days of buyer restart to validate the business case and secure budget approval.
